title: 10 best herbal decoctions for acne
intro_desc: Herbal decoctions for acne are a natural remedy made by steeping herbs in hot water to create a soothing, antiseptic liquid that helps to clear the skin of impurities and reduce inflammation. This treatment targets the root causes of acne, including excess oil production, bacteria growth, and hormonal imbalances. Examples include teas made from green tea, chamomile, and licorice root, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help to calm redness and swelling. By incorporating herbal decoctions into their skincare routine, individuals can experience a significant reduction in acne breakouts, leading to clearer, healthier-looking skin and increased confidence.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Germander decoctions helps with acne because of its potent antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The decoction's active compounds, including terpenoids and flavonoids, penetrate deep into the skin, targeting the root causes of acne such as clogged pores and inflamed follicles. By reducing inflammation and killing bacteria that cause breakouts, germander decoctions can help clear up existing pimples and prevent new ones from forming, resulting in a clearer, healthier complexion.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Germander's anti-inflammatory property helps to reduce redness, swelling, and inflammation associated with acne, making it an effective natural remedy for soothing and calming irritated skin.

Antibacterial property: The antibacterial properties of germander decoction help to combat the bacteria that cause acne, such as Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes), by inhibiting their growth and reducing their ability to produce inflammation-causing toxins.

Antioxidant property: Germander's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells and lead to the formation of acne, while also protecting the skin from environmental stressors and pollutants.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in antibacterial and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in treating acne by reducing inflammation and fighting bacterial infections.
Barks: The barks contain flavonoids and terpenes that have been shown to inhibit the production of sebum, a key factor in acne development, while also exhibiting antimicrobial properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 tablespoons of dried teucrium chamaedrys leaves and stems from a reputable source.
Grind the herbs into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for 30 seconds.
Combine 1 teaspoon of the ground herb mixture with 1 cup boiling water in a glass jar for 10 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into another container discarding the solids after 5 minutes.
Store the decoction in the refrigerator for up to 3 days and consume 2 cups per day as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold decoctions helps with acne because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The decocotion's flavonoids and phenolic acids work to reduce redness and swelling, calming irritated skin and preventing further inflammation. Additionally, the decoction's antibacterial properties help to combat P. acnes bacteria, which can cause clogged pores and acne lesions. As a result, pot marigold decoctions can effectively soothe and heal acne-prone skin, promoting a clear and radiant complexion.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Pot marigold's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, help reduce redness and swelling associated with acne, calming the skin and reducing irritation.

Antibacterial properties: The decoction's antibacterial components, like calophyllolide and flavonoids, inhibit the growth of Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes), a bacteria that contributes to acne development, reducing the risk of infection and promoting healing.

Antioxidant properties: Pot marigold's rich antioxidant content, including vitamins C and E, helps neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells and contribute to acne, protecting the skin from oxidative stress and promoting overall health.
remedy_parts: Roots: Potent in reducing inflammation and swelling, making them effective for soothing acne-prone skin.
Seeds: Rich in antioxidants and fatty acids, seeds help to reduce sebum production and prevent clogged pores.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula officinalis flowers and wash them thoroughly under cold running water.
Combine 20 grams of dried calendula flowers with 500ml of boiling distilled water in a heat-resistant glass container.
Simmer the mixture for 30 minutes at low heat to extract the active compounds from the plant material.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.
Allow the liquid to cool completely before storing it in the refrigerator for up to 72 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage decoctions helps with acne because of its potent antibacterial properties, which effectively combat Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes) bacteria that cause inflammation and clog pores. The antioxidants in sage also reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, calming the skin and preventing further breakouts. Additionally, sage's astringent properties help to shrink enlarged pores, allowing for clearer and more even-toned skin. By incorporating sage decoctions into one's skincare routine, individuals can expect a reduction in acne severity and improved overall skin health.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial property: Sage decoction's antibacterial properties can help combat Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes) bacteria, a common cause of acne, by reducing their growth and preventing infection.

Anti-inflammatory property: Sage's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, can reduce redness, swelling, and irritation associated with acne by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines.

Antioxidant property: Sage decoction's antioxidant properties can help neutralize free radicals that damage skin cells and contribute to acne development, promoting overall skin health and reducing oxidative stress.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sage roots have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing redness and swelling associated with acne.
Rhyzomes: Sage rhyzomes contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that help combat bacteria responsible for causing acne.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried salvia officinalis leaves measure 10 grams.
Boil water in a pot at a rolling boil for 10 minutes.
Add the salvia officinalis leaves to the boiling water and steep for 20 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.
Store the herbal decoction in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 72 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe decoctions helps with acne because of its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which work to reduce redness and swelling associated with acne. The aloe vera gel soothes and calms the skin, reducing inflammation and irritation that can exacerbate breakouts. Additionally, the antibacterial agents in aloe help to combat Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes) bacteria, a common cause of acne, thereby reducing the appearance of acne scars and preventing future breakouts. As a result, herbal aloe decoctions provide a natural and effective solution for managing acne-prone skin.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Herbal aloe decoctions contain compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause acne, such as Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes).

Anti-inflammatory properties: The gel of the aloe plant contains anti-inflammatory compounds like polysaccharides and saponins, which help reduce inflammation and swelling associated with acne, leading to faster healing.

Wound-healing properties: Herbal aloe decoctions have been found to accelerate wound healing by stimulating collagen synthesis, improving tissue hydration, and enhancing the migration of fibroblasts (cells involved in tissue repair) – all essential for reducing scarring and promoting healthy skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used for its anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce redness and swelling associated with acne.
Leaves: Contain aloin, a compound that has antibacterial and antifungal properties, making it effective against acne-causing bacteria.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh aloe vera gel from a mature leaf.
Wash the harvested gel thoroughly with distilled water for 10 minutes.
Blend the cleaned gel with 500ml of boiling water in a saucepan.
Simmer the mixture over medium heat for 20-30 minutes or until reduced by half.
Strain and cool the decoction to room temperature before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion decoctions helps with acne because of its potent antimicrobial properties, which effectively combat the bacteria that cause acne. The decoction also has anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce redness and swelling associated with acne. Additionally, dandelion's detoxifying effects help to flush out toxins and excess hormones from the skin, which can contribute to acne. As a result, regular consumption of dandelion decoctions may lead to a noticeable reduction in acne severity and frequency, promoting clear and healthy-looking skin.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory effects: Dandelion's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as taraxasterol and sesquiterpenoids, help reduce inflammation in the skin, which can alleviate redness and swelling associated with acne.

Antimicrobial activity: The antimicrobial properties of dandelion decoctions can help combat bacterial overgrowth on the skin, reducing the likelihood of clogged pores and preventing the spread of acne-causing infections.

Detoxification support: Dandelion's diuretic and choleretic properties stimulate urine production and bile flow, promoting the elimination of toxins from the body, including those that can contribute to acne.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce inflammation and prevent clogged pores, making them effective in treating acne.
Leaves: The leaves contain salicylic acid, a beta hydroxy acid that helps exfoliate the skin, unclog pores, and reduce acne-causing bacteria.
remedy_recipe: Gather one cup of fresh or dried taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers.
Rinse the herb material gently with clean water and remove any debris.
Combine the rinsed herb material with 2 cups of boiling water in a saucepan.
Reduce heat to simmer for 20-30 minutes or until the liquid has reduced slightly.
Strain the decoction through cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile decoctions helps with acne because of its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The apigenin present in chamomile has been shown to reduce inflammation, which is a major contributor to acne. Additionally, the flavonoids and terpenoids in chamomile have antimicrobial effects that help combat Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes) bacteria, a common cause of acne. By reducing inflammation and killing bacteria, chamomile decoctions can help to soothe and clear up acne-prone skin, leaving it looking healthier and more radiant.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, help reduce redness and swelling associated with acne, promoting calm and soothing skin.

Antimicrobial properties: Chamomile's antimicrobial agents, including terpenoids and flavonoids, inhibit the growth of bacteria that can cause acne, such as Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes), reducing the risk of infection and breakouts.

Soothing and calming effects: Chamomile's flavonoids and volatile oils have a sedative effect on the skin, helping to reduce stress and anxiety, which can contribute to acne, and promoting a sense of calm and relaxation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of chamomile contain high amounts of apigenin, an anti-inflammatory compound that helps soothe and calm acne-prone skin.
Flowers: Chamomile flowers have potent antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to the development of acne.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers and leaves in the morning when dew drops are still present.
Rinse 20g of harvested material with cold water to remove dirt and debris.
Combine rinsed plant material with 500ml of boiling distilled water in a heat-resistant container.
Steep for 30 minutes, allowing the mixture to cool slightly before straining through cheesecloth or a coffee filter.
Discard solids and store cooled decoction in an amber glass bottle at room temperature for up to 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild geranium decoctions helps with acne because of its potent antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The decoction's ability to reduce inflammation and fight off bacteria that cause pimples and acne can lead to a significant reduction in acne lesions. Additionally, wild geranium has natural antiseptic and antimicrobial properties that help to clean the skin, unclog pores, and regulate sebum production, thereby preventing future breakouts. By incorporating wild geranium decoctions into one's skincare routine, individuals may experience a noticeable improvement in their acne-prone skin.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The decoction's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce redness, swelling, and irritation associated with acne, allowing for faster healing of blemishes and prevention of new breakouts.

Antimicrobial properties: Wild geranium decoctions contain antimicrobial compounds that target Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes), the bacteria that causes acne, reducing its growth and helping to prevent future breakouts.

Rhyzomes: Contains flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial properties, helping to reduce acne breakouts.
remedy_recipe: Measure two teaspoons of dried geranium maculatum flowers into a heat-resistant glass bowl.
Boil one cup of distilled water in a pot and pour it over the geranium flowers.
Allow the mixture to steep for 15 minutes, then strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve.
Combine the resulting decoction with two tablespoons of cooled boiled water and let it sit for 30 minutes before use.
Store the prepared decoction in an amber glass bottle at room temperature for up to three days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm decoctions helps with acne because it contains rosmarinic acid, a potent antioxidant that reduces inflammation in the skin. This natural anti-inflammatory agent soothes redness and swelling associated with acne, while also combating P. acnes bacteria that cause breakouts. remedy_recipe: Gather dried melissa officinalis flowers and measure out one teaspoon per cup of boiling water.
Pour the measured amount of boiling water over the dried flowers in a heat-resistant container.
Allow the mixture to steep for 15-20 minutes, then strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve.
Transfer the decoction to a clean glass bottle and store it in the refrigerator for up to three days.
Use the melissa officinalis decoction as a toner by applying it to the face with cotton pads two to three times daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's wort decoctions helps with acne because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The herb's active compounds, such as hyperforin and hypericin, have been shown to reduce inflammation in the skin, which is often a contributing factor to acne. remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality dried hypericum perforatum herbs in sufficient quantity for desired yield.
Measure out one teaspoon of the dried herb per cup of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 15-20 minutes to allow optimal extraction of bioactive compounds.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean glass container.
Allow the decoction to cool and store it in the refrigerator for up to three days before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon decoctions helps with acne because they possess antiseptic, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties that work synergistically to combat acne-causing bacteria and reduce inflammation. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lemons or purchase organic dried lemon peel for 2 tablespoons of material.
Combine 2 cups of water with the dried lemon peel in a stainless steel pot and bring to boil.
Reduce heat to low and simmer mixture for 10-15 minutes to extract essential oils and compounds.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass jar and discard solids.
Store the prepared decoction in the refrigerator for up to 72 hours or freeze for later use.

remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried peppermint leaves and place them in a heat-resistant cup.
Add one cup of boiling water to the cup and let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the liquid using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into another cup discarding the solids.
Let the decoction cool down to room temperature before refrigerating it for up to 24 hours.
Use the cooled decoction as a toner by applying it directly to the affected area with a cotton pad.

remedy_recipe: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried artemisia absinthium leaves and stems, free from contaminants.
Combine the dried herb with 1 quart of boiling water in a large glass or ceramic pot.
Simmer the mixture for 10-15 minutes on low heat, allowing the active compounds to infuse.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container, discarding solids.
Allow the decoction to cool before storing it in the refrigerator for up to 3 days or freezing for longer term use.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh urtica dioica leaves and stems from a trusted source.
Rinse the plant material thoroughly with filtered water to remove dirt debris.
Combine 20-30 grams of urtica dioica plant material with 500 milliliters of boiling water.
Simmer the mixture for 10-15 minutes then strain it through a cheesecloth or coffee filter.
Allow the decoction to cool and store it in an airtight container in the refrigerator.

remedy_recipe: Harvest dried marshmallow root powder according to manufacturer's instructions for acne-prone skin.
Measure two teaspoons of dried marshmallow root powder and add it to one quart of boiling water.
Reduce heat to a simmer and let the mixture steep for 10-15 minutes allowing for maximum nutrient extraction.
Strain the decoction using cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container discarding solids.

Measure out one tablespoon of the herb material for every eight ounces of boiling water.

Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container discarding the solids.

remedy_recipe: Gather one teaspoon of dried gaultheria procumbens leaves and stems.
Combine the herb with two cups of boiling water in a saucepan.
Reduce heat and simmer for 10-15 minutes to allow flavors to meld together.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.
Let the decoction cool completely before refrigerating or freezing for later use.

remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried pimenta dioica leaves and add it to a clean glass cup.
Pour one cup of boiling water over the herb and let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the mixture using a coffee filter or cheesecloth into a separate container, discarding solids.
Allow the decoction to cool down to room temperature, taking about 30 minutes.
Use the cooled herbal liquid as a spot treatment or apply it to affected areas with a cotton pad.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice decoctions helps with acne because of its potent antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The glycyrrhizin present in licorice root has been shown to inhibit the growth of Propionibacterium acnes, a bacterium that causes acne. remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory activity: Licorice root's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce redness and swelling associated with acne by inhibiting prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that cause inflammation.

Hormone regulation: Licorice root's glycosides, particularly glycyrrhizin, have been shown to inhibit 5-alpha-reductase, an enzyme that converts testosterone into dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone linked to acne development, thereby regulating hormonal imbalances and reducing acne severity.

remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root powder into a heat-resistant glass cup.
Boil one cup of filtered water in a kettle or on the stovetop and pour it over the powdered root.
Simmer the mixture for 10-15 minutes, allowing the active compounds to extract from the root.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean glass container, discarding the solids.

remedy_recipe: Harvest equisetum arvense plants with spores and cut them into 2-3 inch pieces weighing approximately 20 grams.
Rinse the plant material with cold water then soak it in distilled water for 30 minutes.
Boil the soaked plant material in 400 milliliters of distilled water at 100 degrees celsius for 10 minutes.
Simmer the decoction at a temperature of 60-70 degrees celsius for an additional 20 minutes to extract active compounds.
Strain and discard solids then store the decoction in a clean glass container refrigerated at 2-8 degrees celsius for up to 24 hours before use.

remedy_recipe: Harvest malva sylvestris leaves and flowers in late summer or early fall for maximum potency.

Combine 2 teaspoons of chopped malva sylvestris with 1 cup of boiling distilled water and steep for 15 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container discarding solids.
Allow the decoction to cool then transfer it to an amber glass bottle and store in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.

remedy_recipe: Gather 10-20 grams of dried silybum marianum aerial parts and place them in a clean glass container.
Add 500 milliliters of boiling water to the container and let it steep for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a large bowl, squeezing gently to extract maximum essence.
Discard the solids and transfer the decoction to a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid, storing it in the refrigerator at a temperature below 8°c for up to 72 hours.
Drink 250 milliliters of the cooled decoction twice daily, preferably an hour before meals, for a duration of 14 days to alleviate acne symptoms effectively.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat acne with herbal decoctions is by brewing a mixture of herbs that target acne-causing bacteria and inflammation. Combine 1 tablespoon of dried calendula flowers, 1 tablespoon of dried tea tree leaves, and 2 tablespoons of dried chamomile flowers in boiling water. Steep for 10-15 minutes, then strain and apply the cooled decoction as a face mask or add to your bathwater for a soothing soak. This natural remedy helps reduce acne and promotes clear skin.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to acne that are treated with herbal decoctions are eczema, rosacea, and dermatitis. Herbal decoctions containing ingredients like turmeric, ginger, and licorice root can help soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing inflammation and redness. Other herbs like neem, tea tree, and chamomile may be used to treat acne-like symptoms such as hyperpigmentation and scarring. These natural remedies promote healthy skin and reduce the appearance of blemishes and imperfections.
